questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among classes given below. [SSC (10+2) 2013] Mammal, Dog, Rat

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Which number is common to all the geometrical figures in the diagram below?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Read the following diagram and find the region representing persons who are educated and employed but not confirmed. [SSC (CGL) 2013] Educated, Employed, Confirmed in job

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In a class of 46 students, 18 played football, 17 played cricket including 6 who played football. 16 students played hockey including 4 who played cricket but not football. Five students played football but no out door games. Which of the following diagrams represents these facts?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the following diagram, square represents the area where jackfruit trees are grown, circle represents mango trees and triangle represents coconut trees. Which numbers represent the area where all types of trees are grown? [SSC (CPO) 2012]

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Which numbered space in the diagram below represents doctors who are players as well as artists? Players, Doctors, Artists

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: Study the following diagram and answer the question given below. [SSC (CPO) 2013] Teachers, Singers, Authors How many teachers are neither authors nor singers?

questions.subject: non_verbal
questions.body: In the given diagram, circle represents strong men, square represents short men and triangle represents military officers. Which region represents military officers who are short but not strong? [SSC (Multitasking) 2013]
